title: Hemis Monastery
tagline: Hemis Monastery — spiritual town of Ladakh (UT)

overview.short: Hemis Monastery is a Himalayan Buddhist monastery (gompa) of the Drukpa Lineage, in Hemis on the bank of the Indus River, Ladakh, India.
overview.description: Hemis Monastery is a Himalayan Buddhist monastery (gompa) of the Drukpa Lineage, in Hemis on the bank of the Indus River, Ladakh, India. Situated 45 km from Leh, it was re-established in 1672 by the Ladakhi king Sengge Namgyal. The annual Hemis festival honouring Padmasambhava is held there in early June. Hemis village is located 40 km southeast of Leh on Leh-Manali Highway and under-construction Bhanupli–Leh line.

topPlaces.name: Hemis
topPlaces.description: Hemis, also spelled Hamis, is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Kharu tehsil, 40 km southeast of Leh town on the Leh-Manali Highway and under-construction Bhanupli–Leh line.

topPlaces.name: Changa, Ladakh
topPlaces.description: Changa is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Kharu tehsil, on the bank of the Indus River.

topPlaces.name: Chuchot Shama
topPlaces.description: Chuchot Shama is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India. It is located in the Leh tehsil.

topPlaces.name: Karu, Ladakh
topPlaces.description: Kharu (Wylie: mkhar bu, THL: khar bu) also spelt Karu, is a town, subdivision, tehsil, block, tourist station and military base on the bank of Indus River in the Leh district of Union territory of Ladakh in India. It is located 34 km southeast of Leh on Leh-Manali Highway and under-construction Bhanupli–Leh line.

topPlaces.name: Stakna
topPlaces.description: Stakna is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India, on the banks of the Indus River. The Stakna Monastery is located there.

Kharu (Wylie: mkhar bu, THL: khar bu) also spelt Karu, is a town, subdivision, tehsil, block, tourist station and military base on the bank of Indus River in the Leh district of Union territory of Ladakh in India. It is located 34 km southeast of Leh on Leh-Manali Highway and under-construction Bhanupli–Leh line.
Stakna is a village in the Leh district of Ladakh, India, on the banks of the Indus River. The Stakna Monastery is located there.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Hemis Monastery?
faq.a: The best time to visit Hemis Monastery is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Hemis Monastery?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kushok Bakula Rimpochee Airport, Leh (29 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (275 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Hemis Monastery?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Hemis Monastery at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Hemis Monastery?
faq.a: Stays in Hemis Monastery start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Hemis Monastery?
faq.a: The top attractions are Hemis, Changa, Ladakh, Chuchot Shama, Karu, Ladakh, Stakna.
